notes: Synchronous slash command (no LLM call) for Gemini CLI. SETTLED by the live recording: across /stats and /help the per-session JSONL transcript holds EXACTLY the warm-up exchange (1 user line + 1 'gemini' reply) — neither slash command persists a line, so the parser never opens a turn for them (parser.go only opens a turn on a type=user text prompt). Unlike claudecode there is no <local-command> wrapper to filter; Gemini never persists the slash invocation at all. The recipe issues a warm-up turn FIRST so the per-session transcript materializes (Gemini only writes the file once a real user message lands; without it resolve_transcript can't bind), then /stats and /help inside a sleep-held window. SESSION IDENTITY (two-tier): a transient proc-<PID> presession is born ready (presession_birth); the rollout session reconciles with a different session_id keyed on the transcript header (session_birth, new_session); turn_start/turn_end anchor to the rollout session, never the presession. slash_window pins ready with duration_at_least_ms:1800 + invariants forbidding any state_transition to working/waiting — what would fire if /stats or /help (incorrectly) drove a model call; they don't. WINDOW BOUND (1800ms, not the recipe's full ~8s): the daemon emits a spurious ~3ms 'force ready→working on first activity' flicker at ~+2006ms after the warm-up turn-end — this is daemon TEARDOWN bookkeeping (it fires right after the presession proc-<PID> transcript_removed, NOT a slash effect: the transcript proves no slash line was ever written), so the window is bounded to before that teardown flicker. The flicker is a known daemon teardown artifact, orthogonal to this scenario's observable (slash = no model turn). observations.model is the live API-key-tier default gemini-3.5-flash (the AI-Studio key pool model — NOT the OAuth-tier gemini-3-flash-preview the doc-based assess guessed); cost+tokens nonzero from the warm-up turn.
source: .specs/agent-scenarios.md → Feature: Synchronous slash command (no LLM call)

text: proc-<PID> pre-session appears in ready shortly after launch
Rollout session reconciles (different session_id, keyed on the transcript header sessionId), born ready
Warm-up prompt → working on the rollout session (real LLM turn; materializes the transcript so subsequent slash steps run against a bound session)
Warm-up turn completes back to ready

State stays ready while /stats and /help are issued — neither synchronous slash command drives a working transition (the transcript holds only the warm-up exchange; window bounded before the daemon teardown flicker)
